arguments.query: SSO session loss after password reset

articles.title: SSO Session Loss: Root Cause Analysis

article.title: SSO Session Loss: Root Cause Analysis
article.body: Root cause: SSO session storage layer fails to handle password-reset-triggered session invalidation correctly. When a user resets their password, the old session token is not properly invalidated before the new session is created, resulting in a race condition that drops the session entirely.

final_answer: The likely root cause is a race condition in the SSO session storage layer: when a user resets their password, the old session token is not properly invalidated before the new session is created. This causes the session to be dropped entirely. Recommendation: fix the session invalidation ordering in the password reset flow.

messages.content: Investigate why users lose SSO session after password reset
